title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 25 - 2017: BouldeRoman Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder about sobering up due to running out of money, and explains a Roman-calendar-based genealogical timeline he's building for his website and TV show concept. He describes a generational cycle system (every 33 years) inspired by Herodotus and a book about generational patterns, applied to a single fictional family spanning thousands of years.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Being sober makes complex creative work like the timeline easier to manage
A big unfinished website makes the creator look crazy; polishing is the priority for the rest of the year
33-year generations and their cyclical patterns are a legitimate and fascinating framework for understanding history
Reluctant to go further into debt; trying to avoid credit card and borrowing from mom again

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log. Right now it's 12.45 April 25th. This will be April 25th video.
I don't remember which day was the last one.
I'm more or less sober now.
I pretty much sobered up because I ran out of money.
I can't buy any more weed.
I could go into credit card debt, but I don't want to go into credit card debt again.
And I can't borrow any more money. I don't feel right about borrowing more money from my mom
Because I got $12,500 for her this winter. I'm not supposed to talk about that.
So I'm kind of getting painted into a corner here.
But I think I can do a better job, probably, with my timeline if I'm sober
Because it's a little bit complicated.
Like, it's a lot more complicated than what I'm used to.
There's decisions I have to make, artistic decisions,
Because the timeline was never finished.
It was just like halfway through.
I was still on a designing stage when my car broke down in 2011.
And that's when I pretty much abandoned it.
I worked on it again in the latter half of 2012 and the beginning of 2013.
But that was just stuff that I was already working on.
It was like tribes in China.
Anyways, so I was working on that yesterday.
The idea was to work full force on my website until the end of April.
And so I have one more week.
This week I'm going to go full bore and see what I can do until I...
And then for next week, you know, I'll be sober.
So we'll just think about coming up for air and doing more, like,
Well-rounded life maintenance type of stuff, like selling my car.
I forgot to turn these lights on.
Is going to help me sell my car.
She even offered me to drive it to her house.
My mom didn't want me to lower the price.
That's maybe one reason nobody's interested in my car.
So yesterday, I did do a new thing on my website.
It's this thing that I was thinking about forever.
So in 2011, I was going to do it.
I have my timeline set up.
Maybe if I remember, I'll put the link.
If not, just go to my Boulder Room page.
Just go to Education, Boulder Room, and then Timeline, Boulder Room, Timeline.
And I'll send me the time chart.
That's later, to align the dates.
Sorry, I'm jumping all over the place.
So if you go on Wikipedia and you type like 485 BC,
It'll also tell you what year in the Roman calendar was.
Because that's the calendar we all use.
You know, the year 244 in the Roman calendar.
Because I'm starting at the same date.
Every 33 years, I created a generation.
And I wanted to do this before,
But I was listening to, uh...
And they were talking about some dude.
Yeah, they believe in the generational, um...
And that we're the bad generation.
And I went and I Googled it.
And these guys wrote a book about...
For like the last thousand years or something,
They have each generation with a name and a characteristic.
Every four generations, it cycles back to the same.
So it's like, you know, like...
And I guess we're like the lazy generations.
Those are like the hard workers or something.
Because that's what Herodotus used as what he considered a generation.
And he predicted how far back the priests of Sace went.
Because they had priests, 300, around their temple.
Yeah, and like my mom was 33 when I was born.
It's a good date to have kids.
So, in my little timeline here, I have...
Because it's one family for my television show.
He each has his kid when he's 33.
So, he knows his grandkid until...
His grandkid's 33 and then he dies.
You know, each person would know his grandfather until they're 33.
And that kind of a thing.
And so, when I do the show, I would act out the mid-section.
And you can actually see it illustrated.
Because the mid-third is pretty much the size of their names.
So, I would be like Ambustus, you know.
And then when he turns 66, I become Brutus when he's 33 until 36.
So, everybody in the private of their life.
And then the other guys would kind of become, I guess, secondary figures.
But what I just said sounds totally confusing.
Because it's pretty out of the box.
Or I will explain it there.
But it'll make sense once I explain it.
And, like, have it organized and everything.
The danger with a big website like mine is that when you build it all the same and none of it's done, then nobody's going to get it.
And they're just going to think you're crazy.
You know, and you're going to be like me where you have only 10 people on the entire planet at any given time on your website.
And 75% of the people are there for the very first time.
So, that's what I'll be doing the rest of the year.
Because, like, I'm literally learning the history of Rome just by building this timeline.
Anyways, I think I've talked long enough.
It's pretty much like my first sober video.
I talk a lot faster when I'm sober and louder so people can understand me more and I probably make more sense.
So, I'll try to put a green screen up.
I don't like doing my YouTube videos with just this green background.
I'd rather just put stuff back there.
But it might not look all that good, all of it, because I forgot to turn the lights on.
So, I'll have to fast forward it to the part where the light's on when I do the green screen stuff.
